data.title: Columbus McKinnon prices senior notes and loans
data.description:
•  Columbus McKinnon (CMCO) priced its offering of $900M in aggregate principal amount of 7.125% senior secured notes due 2033 in connection with the company's previously announced pending acquisition of Kito Crosby.
  • The aggregate size of the offering has been downsized from $1.225M to $900M.
  • The offering is expected to close on January 30, 2026.
  • Besides, the company also priced a new $1.65B aggregate principal amount senior secured term loan B due in 2033. The loans under the facility will be issued at a price equal to 99.0% of their face value and bear interest at SOFR plus 3.50%.
  • Both net proceeds will be used to fund the Kito Crosby acquisition, including settling its debts, alongside sales of preferred shares to CD&R XII Keystone Holdings, L.P., and a new credit agreement and refinancing existing company debt.

data.title: BancFirst GAAP EPS of $1.75 misses by $0.04, revenue of $181M beats by $7.24M
data.description:
• BancFirst press release (BANF): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.75 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $181M (+11.1% Y/Y) beats by $7.24M.
  • Net interest margin improved slightly to 3.71% for the fourth quarter of 2025 from 3.68% for the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • The company recorded a reversal of provision for credit losses on loans of $2.0 million in the three months ending December 31, 2025 compared to a reversal of $1.4 million for the same period in 2024.
  • Asset quality was strong through the quarter. Nonaccrual loans of $61.1 million represented 0.72% of total loans at December 31, 2025, relatively unchanged from $58.0 million or 0.72% of total loans at year-end 2024.
  • The allowance for credit losses to total loans was 1.22% at December 31, 2025, down slightly from 1.24% at December 31, 2024.

data.title: Associated Banc-Corp GAAP EPS of $0.80 beats by $0.09, revenue of $389.36M beats by $7.04M
data.description:
• Associated Banc-Corp press release (ASB): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.80 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $389.36M (+513.0% Y/Y) beats by $7.04M.
  • Fourth quarter 2025 period end deposits of $35.6 billion were up 2%, or $671 million, from the prior quarter and were up 3%, or $904 million from the same period last year. 
  • Core customer deposits1 increased $691 million from the prior quarter and increased $989 million from the same period last year to $29.6 billion.
  • The fourth quarter 2025 provision for credit losses was $7 million, compared to a provision of $16 million in the prior quarter and a provision of $17 million in the same period last year. 
  • The allowance for credit losses on loans of $419 million increased $5 million from the prior quarter and increased $17 million from the same period last year. 
  • The Company's capital position remains strong, with a CET1 capital ratio of 10.49% at December 31, 2025. 
  • In 2026, we expect period end total deposit growth of 5% to 6% and period end core customer deposit growth of 5% to 6% as compared to the year ended December 31, 2025, excluding any impact from the acquisition of American National Corporation.
  • "We expect total net interest income growth of 5.5% to 6.5% in 2026, excluding any impact from the acquisition of American National Corporation."
  • "We expect total noninterest income growth of between 4% and 5% in 2026, excluding any impact from the acquisition of American National Corporation."
  • "We expect total noninterest expense to grow by 3% in 2026, excluding any impact from the acquisition of American National Corporation."
  • "In 2026, we expect the annual effective tax rate to be between 19% and 21%, assuming no change in the corporate tax rate and excluding any impact from the acquisition of American National Corporation."

data.title: Ambiq Micro prices upsized offering at $31 per share
Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson tops Q4 estimates; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• Telefonaktiebolaget LM Ericsson press release (ERIC): Q4 GAAP EPS of SEK 2.57 beats by SEK 1.17.
  • Revenue of SEK 69.3B (-4.9% Y/Y) beats by SEK 2.26B.
  • Organic sales grew by 6% YoY.
  • Adjusted1 gross margin was 48.0% (vs. 46.3% last year) driven mainly by improvements in Cloud Software and Services.
  • Adjusted1 EBITA was SEK 12.7B (vs. 10.2B last year). with an 18.3% (vs. 14.1% last year) margin.
  • Free cash flow before M&A was SEK 14.9B (vs. 15.8B last year).
  • A dividend of SEK 3.00 (2.85) per share for 2025 and a share buyback program of SEK 15.0B. will be proposed to the AGM by the Board of Directors.
  • Outlook: Amortization of intangible assets is expected to be around SEK -0.5B per quarter, of which approximately SEK -0.4B related to segment Enterprise.
  • Restructuring charges for 2026 are expected to be at elevated levels.
  • Segments: Increased uncertainty remains on the outlook, both in terms of potential for further tariff changes and in the broader macroeconomic environment. Networks Sales growth in Q1 2026 is expected to be broadly similar to 3-year average seasonality. Adjusted gross margin in Q1 2026 is expected to be in the range of 49% to 51%. Cloud Software and Services Sales growth in Q1 2026 is expected to be below 3-year average seasonality. Enterprise: In 2025, the contribution to Enterprise results from divested and deconsolidated operations was SEK 2.7B in net sales, SEK 1.8B in adjusted gross income, and SEK 8.8B in adjusted EBITA.

data.title: Provident Financial announces 5% buyback
data.description:
• Provident Financial (PROV), the holding company for Provident Savings Bank, announced a new stock repurchase program for up to 5% of its common stock, ~318,875 shares.
  • Additionally, the January 2025 stock repurchase program is canceled effective January 23, 2026. There were 16,825 remaining shares eligible for repurchase in the January 2025 stock repurchase program that will no longer be repurchased.

data.title: Sun Pharma cleared to sell generic Wegovy, intensifying India’s obesity-drug race with Novo Nordisk
data.description:
• India’s leading drugmaker by revenue, Sun Pharmaceutical Industries, has received regulatory approval to manufacture and sell semaglutide, the generic version of the blockbuster weight-loss drug Wegovy, in India.
  • The drugmaker plans to launch generic semaglutide, the active ingredient in Novo Nordisk's (NVO) Wegovy and its blockbuster diabetes drug Ozempic, under the brand name Noveltreat for weight loss once the semaglutide patent expires in March, it said in a statement.
  • The product has received approval from the DCGI following a review of a Phase III clinical trial conducted in India. 
  • Demand for weight-loss medications is surging in India, as competitors Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO) vie for market share.

data.title: Short interest in Li Auto surges to record 9.6% amid intense premium SUV competition - report
data.description:
Li Auto (LI) bearish bets are said to be jumping to record levels as the company struggles to fend off rivals in the premium SUV market after earning a reputation as one of Asia’s most profitable short trades last year.

Wagers against the Hong Kong-listed stock climbed to a fresh high of 9.6% of free float as of Wednesday, up from around 1% a year earlier, Bloomberg reported Friday, citing S&P Global data.

The firm has been the most shorted Chinese automaker since September, Bloomberg-compiled figures show.

Over the past year, Li Auto’s (LI) shares have slid roughly 25%, even as Hong Kong equities broadly advanced, turning the stock into a popular target for short sellers.

 

 

The automaker was the second-most profitable short in Asia-Pacific last year, trailing only Chinese food delivery giant Meituan, according to S3 Partners data. Meituan CEO Wang Xing was once Li Auto's largest external shareholder.

"Shorting demand is expected to remain elevated but volatile as Li Auto navigates a strong competitive environment," said Matt Chessum, director of securities finance at S&P Global Market Intelligence, according to Bloomberg.

Li Auto (LI) reported December 2025 vehicle deliveries of 44,246 units, bringing fourth-quarter deliveries to 109,194 vehicles. This pushed the company’s cumulative deliveries past the 1.5 million mark, reaching 1,540,215 units by year-end. Despite a sequential recovery in December, deliveries fell 24.4% year-over-year, marking the seventh consecutive month of annual contraction for the company.

Related tickers: VanEck Low Carbon Energy ETF (SMOG), State Street SPDR S&P Kensho Smart Mobility ETF (HAIL), Great Wall Motor Company (GWLLF).

data.title: Australia manufacturing gains momentum while services growth hits a 4-year peak of 56.0 in January
data.description:
The S&P Global Australia Manufacturing PMI rose to 52.4 in January 2026 from 51.6 in December, according to flash estimates, signaling continued expansion. 

The S&P Global Flash Australia Services PMI Business Activity Index rose to 56.0 in January from 51.1 in December, indicating the fastest expansion since early 2022 in the service sector if confirmed. 

The S&P Global Flash Australia Composite PMI rose to 55.5 in January from 51.0 in December, signaling expansion for a sixteenth month and hitting its joint-highest level since April 2022, matched by August 2025.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index was largely unchanged around 8,850 in early Friday deals, halting gains from the previous session, as profit-taking in banks offset strength in gold stocks. The Australian dollar held its recent gains around $0.684, trading near a sixteen-month high, as strong jobs data boosted expectations of a near-term rate hike, while upbeat PMI figures further supported sentiment. 

More on Australia:
